title: Post-Quantum Cryptography and Hybrid Migration · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A municipal sensor and safety network. They need explicit roles and device identity, X25519 and ML-KEM hybrid establishment, signed transcript, downgrade defense, labeled key schedule, ordered AEAD records, replay handling, certificate rotation, compatibility telemetry, and rollback. Planned artifact: Deliver Authenticated Hybrid Device Channel with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Authenticate peer identities and ephemeral public keys with signatures, certificates, PSKs, or an established trust channel.
Bind ordered message type, role, version, suite, identities, ephemeral values, session ID, sequence, and prior transcript into each state transition.
Authenticate offered and selected versions, algorithms, parameters, and features and fail closed on unsupported or stripped choices.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Peer Authentication
steps.instruction:
project case — A project cryptographic engineering team handles case 7684 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate hybrid ML-KEM and ML-DSA migration on inventory, specified combiner, transcript binding, downgrade defense, and rollback; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Authenticate peer identities and ephemeral public keys with signatures, certificates, PSKs, or an established trust channel.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Peer Authentication.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for authenticate peer identities and ephemeral public keys with signatures, certificates, psks, or an established trust channel. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Peer Authentication, keep Crypto Go Peer Authentication connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
